message: Not at all.  I’m interested in hearing more about your story.

Well, I found out my writing isn’t as great as I thought it was. Instructors dock marks for sentence structure and care about punctuation.  I told them that I have ADHD and I don’t always pay attention to details, but they didn’t care.
They don’t have to make exceptions for you if they don’t want to.
I found that out the hard way.  I also found out that I need extra time for completing tests.  We write exams in large rooms full of people and it is very distracting.
That sounds very difficult.
I took your advice and booked a meeting with accessibility services, but I should have done it early like you told me to.  By the time I met with them, it was nearing the end of my first term.
Why so long?
Apparently, I’m not the only one who didn’t book early or thought they could do well without supports.
What did you do?
Nothing I could do.  My grades really suffered.
Do you have accommodations in place now?

I do, but I was surprised because they will only give me 50% extra time for writing tests.  In high school the teachers gave me as much time as I wanted.  Being under a time constraint stresses me out.
Why won’t they give you the unlimited time?
They said something about "fairness" to all students, but I don’t think it's fair that there are different rules for different places.
And what about your writing?
They don’t have a specific accommodation for my need, but they recommended I seek out writing services from student services.  I’m on my way there now for my first appointment.
Good luck.  I hope they can help.
Yes, good luck.

Thanks. And thanks Sam for your help last year.  I should have listened to you more, but at least I was able to remember your advice and seek help.  With better supports in place, I should be able to turn things around.
I think so too.  I’ll see you around.
